mnemonics.title: Availability Formula
mnemonics.content: Availability = MTBF / (MTBF + MTTR) - Remember: More MTBF (reliability) and less MTTR (repair time) = Higher availability

mnemonics.title: Response Time Components
mnemonics.content: Response Time = Wait Time + Service Time - 'WWS' (Wait Wait Serve) to remember queue and processing both count
mnemonics.usage: Understanding where delays occur in request processing

visualTips.visualization: Think of 'nines': 99% = 2 nines = 3.65 days downtime/year; 99.9% = 3 nines = 8.76 hours; 99.99% = 4 nines = 52.6 minutes; 99.999% = 5 nines = 5.26 minutes
visualTips.purpose: Visualize how each '9' dramatically reduces downtime

visualTips.visualization: Imagine water flowing through pipes - the narrowest pipe limits flow. System throughput is limited by the slowest/most constrained component
U-curve: Too low (wasted resources) → Optimal zone (70-80%) → Too high (congestion/bottleneck) → Failure

analogies.analogy: A highway toll booth - number of cars passing through per minute is throughput
Time from when you request a pizza to when it arrives at your door
A narrowed road section that limits how many cars can pass regardless of road width elsewhere
Your body's normal temperature - used to detect if you're sick (deviation from baseline)
Adding more cash counters during holiday shopping vs upgrading existing counters to faster processors
